name: Shredded Pork with Yu Shon Sauce
recipeInstructions:
Note: Pork should be trimmed of fat.
Soak wood ears in warm water until softened, about half an hour. Rinse well, then shred. Cut pork into paper-thin slices and shred. Crush water chestnuts with a heavy cleaver and shred. Combine pork, 2 teaspoons soy sauce, cornstarch, and egg, and mix well. The meat mixture should be moist. If needed, add a little of the cornstarch-water solution. Combine 2 tablespoons soy sauce, garlic, ginger, sugar, broth, and vinegar. Heat oil in a wok or large skillet. Add pork mixture, wood ears, and water chestnuts, and cook, stirring gently, just until meat changes color. Immediately pour meat and vegetables into a large strainer set over a bowl to drain off oil. Return 1 to 2 tablespoons oil to the pan. Mix hot bean paste with oil in the pan. Add meat mixture and stir-fry. Add soy sauce mixture and continue cooking. Stir in green onion. Add remaining cornstarch solution and stir. Mix in sesame oil and turn out onto a serving plate.
